mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XE Project 8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Jaguar XE Project 8 engine (AJ133 5.0 SC) typically costs £7,500–£12,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. These are specialist-only replacements — the 600PS supercharged V8 requires Jaguar V8 expertise. A timing chain job alone costs £2,000–£4,500.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XE Project 8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0L V8 is fundamentally robust, but early examples (pre-2015) are known for timing chain guide wear — the defining issue of the engine family. The Project 8's 600PS tune puts additional stress on the supercharger and cooling systems. The single most important factor is oil change frequency — 5,000–7,500 mile intervals are essential. JLR revised the timing chain guides in 2015 with steel inserts.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XE Project 8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide wear (pre-2015 design, cold-start rattle, £2,000–£4,500), plastic coolant crossover pipe failure (coolant loss, overheating, £800–£2,000), and supercharger coupler wear (rattling at idle, £500–£1,000). Cooling system components are a particular concern on the 600PS tune.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XE Project 8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Project 8 values range from £80,000–£150,000+ depending on condition and mileage. A £7,500–£12,000 reconditioned engine represents 5–15% of vehicle value — well within our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold. For almost any example, replacement is financially justified.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XE Project 8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Project 8 uses the AJ133 engine code — Jaguar Land Rover's 5.0-litre supercharged V8, tuned to 600PS for this application. This is the most powerful version of the AJ133 ever produced. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £2,000–£4,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£7,500–£12,000). JLR revised the guide design in 2015 with steel inserts. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar XE Project 8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ133 5.0 SC engine in Project 8 specification. The 600PS tune means these engines command a premium over standard AJ133 units. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Project 8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Jaguar specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). The supercharger and cooling system add complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XE Project 8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative replacement before failure — always cheaper than engine replacement
Fit aftermarket metal pipes for a permanent fix
Replace as a set — common on high-mileage AJ133s
Replacement often £7,500–£12,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar XE Project 8? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing chain guide history (post-2015 build or retrofit)
* Coolant crossover pipes replaced with metal aftermarket units
* Full service history with regular oil changes (5,000–7,500 miles)
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (crossover pipe failure risk)
* Cold-start rattle without documented timing chain work
* Poor service history — the AJ133 is unforgiving of extended oil change intervals
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Project 8 is a landmark Jaguar — the most powerful road car the company has ever built. But with only 300 examples worldwide and a 600PS supercharged V8, it's a high-stakes ownership proposition. The AJ133 engine is fundamentally robust, but timing chain guides and cooling system components demand vigilance. A post-2015 car with documented timing chain history and metal coolant pipes is the gold standard. Buy with your eyes open, maintain religiously, and the Project 8 will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar XE Project 8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XE Project 8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain guide wear warning)
* Service History — Oil change intervals are critical — 5,000–7,500 miles is the safe zone
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss; inspect around crossover pipes for leaks
* Supercharger Health — Any rattling from the supercharger at idle? (coupler wear)
* Engine Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, flat spots, or rough idle?
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; AJ133s can consume oil in hard use
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
